title: Primary Culture of Nodose Ganglion Neurons from Mouse
author: Diego DFF Fernández-Fernández
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kzxcx7n

steps.title: Deeply anaesthesise the mouse using a...
steps.instruction: Deeply anaesthesise the mouse using a CO 2 chamber or isoflurane overdose. Mice are SWISS CD1 strain 30-60 days old Male and females. 12-h light : 12-h dark cycle Fed ab libitum

steps.title: Check that the animal is anaesthesised assesing foot pad pinch
steps.instruction: Check that the animal is anaesthesised assesing foot pad pinch. If the animal responds to this stimulation, apply further anaesthetic.

steps.title: While anaesthesised, quickly sacrifice the animal by decapitation
steps.instruction: While anaesthesised, quickly sacrifice the animal by decapitation. Position the backwards and pin. Cut medial from the base of the neck to the mouth. Pin the traquea to see the nodose ganglion as an enlargement of the vagus nerve, which goes parallel to the carotid artery (see figure). a Use autoclaved surgery material during all extraction procedures. Material should be kept in 70º alcohol while it is not being used during the procedure. Before use, it has to be wash in destilate water and saline (NaCl 0.9 %) for removal of ganglia.

steps.title: Quickly isolate the ganglion by cutting...
steps.instruction: Quickly isolate the ganglion by cutting it free from the vagus nerve. Do the same procedure with the second, billateral ganglion.

steps.title: Transfer both ganglia to a 35...
steps.instruction: Transfer both ganglia to a 35 mm well filled with 1 mL of cold medium (L-15). Leibovitzs L-15 Medium Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L4386 undetermined, L-15 per well

steps.title: Under magnification, clean both ganglia from...
steps.instruction: Under magnification, clean both ganglia from rests of other tissue, specially blood.

steps.title: The following material must be sterilized...
steps.instruction: The following material must be sterilized and kept in a laminar flow cabinet: Corning wells 35 mm. Tubes 10 mL. Two pasteur glass pipettes (one with a thin polished mouth and another with a broader mouth). L-15 medium without L-glutamine. Hanks’ Balanced Salt Solution (HBSS) + HEPES. HEPES 1 M is added to HBSS to get a final concentration of 10 mM HEPES. Culture medium: 50 mL L-15 supplemented with supplemented with 10% foetal calf serum, 24 mM NaHCO 3 , 38 mM D-glucose, 100 UI/ml penicillin-100 μg/ml streptomicyn, 2 mM L-glutamine and 50 ng/ml nerve growth factor. Keep at 4º C before use. Trypsin: 1 mg/ml in HBSS + 6 mg/mL albumin. Colagenase: 2.5 mg/ml in HBSS + 6 mg/ml albúmin. Laminin: prepare 3 wells with 200 µL of laminin each (10 mg/ml solved in Earle’s Balanced Salts Solution (EBSS)). Before harvesting, drop of laminin must be incubated for at least two hours in the incubator (37 ºC, 95% O 2 /5% CO 2 ).

steps.title: After cleaning for extra tissue, In...
steps.instruction: After cleaning for extra tissue, In the laminar flow cabinet ganglia must be washed 3 times with 2 ml of HBSS + HEPES.

steps.title: Add 2 mL of colagenase solution...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of colagenase solution and incubate during 15 minutes (37 ºC, 95% O 2 /5% CO 2 ). 00:15:00, colagenase

steps.title: Wash again two times with HBSS...
steps.instruction: Wash again two times with HBSS + HEPES to remove colagenase. Add 2 mL of trypsin solution and incubate during 30 minutes (37 ºC, 95% O 2 /5% CO 2 ). 00:30:00, trypsin

steps.title: Take both ganglia with the smallest...
steps.instruction: Take both ganglia with the smallest possible amount of trypsin and place them in a 10 mL tube containing 2 mL medium (FCS in the medium will stop trypsinization). Using the small tip pasteur pipette, gently dissociate the ganglia until they are no longer visible.

steps.title: Transfer the 3 mL cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Transfer the 3 mL cell suspension to another 10 mL tube containing 3 mL L-15. Centrifugate this tube. 00:03:00, Centrifugation: 500 rpm

steps.title: After centrifugation, carefully poor the supernatant out of the tube
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, carefully poor the supernatant out of the tube. Add culture medium to the remaining pellet of neurons (visible as a white spot at the bottom of the tube). undetermined, cultured medium to the tube with the pellet

steps.title: During centrifugation, take the laminin coated...
steps.instruction: During centrifugation, take the laminin coated wells and remove the laminin. Wash the place were the laminin drop was at least 2 times with L-15. Then remove the L-15 and leave the well empty.

steps.title: Seed 200 mL of medium +...
steps.instruction: Seed 200 mL of medium + cells in suspension in the centre of each of the three wells (where the laminin drop was previously adhered).

steps.title: Place the wells with cells in the incubator
steps.instruction: Place the wells with cells in the incubator. Undetermined, 95% O2/5% CO2 02:00:00

steps.title: Carefully, add 2 mL of culture medium to each well
steps.instruction: Carefully, add 2 mL of culture medium to each well. Make sure that the medium is not cold. Keep cells in the incubator during at least 24 hours before the electrophysiological experiments. Undetermined, 95% O2/5% CO2 24:00:00, before electrophysiology
